Updated: 1/1/2002
New episodes of Dragon Ball expeceted in the Spring of 2002.
Season 5 of DBZ has ended, reruns airing on Cartoon Network. New episodes expected Fall 2002.
Movie #6 expected around October 2002. #5 will not be released theatrically, #6 is unlikely.
Infogrames/Wizard Works is currently producing the upcoming DBZ games. First game is "The Legacy of Goku" for Game Boy Advance, expected around March or April 2002.
 
Note: The following dates are provided by the manufactures. Some retailers may receive their shipments early.


DVD

Dragon Ball Z

(1/29/2002)

(1/29/2002)

(1/29/2002)

(1/29/2002)

(1/22/2002)

Sunday, 1/20/2002 @ 9:04 PM PST by: Julian
Community News: You learn something new every day...
Only a few things to report... but I've got a bit of news afterward, so:

  • DBZ Moms has added 8 new fanart images.

  • Future Foretold has put up a new doujinshi for people to look at.

  • Planet Z has put up the fifth chapter of a fanfic called "Crossed Over".

  • Saiyan Brat's Lair has 10 new fanarts up.

In other news, I've found out a connection that I never knew. After perusing FUNimation's Yu Yu Hakusho site, and noticing that they were carrying over the "King Yama" label for Enma Daiou (the ruler of the afterlife), I decided to do some investigating... and it seems that the Enma of east-Asian myth is the same as the Hindu god Yama-Raja (literally "King Yama"), Lord and Judge of the Dead in Indian folklore. Apparently, this was brought to China with Buddhism, and it became "Enma". So... if you care about this sort of thing, FUNimation's dub name for him isn't a mistake; it's just odd that they would use the Hindu name. I've updated the Bios: E page with this info, but I don't have a picture for the character as of yet. Well... I do, but not from DBZ. I'll find one, eventually. Until then, just stare at the nice default picture that means "image not found".

Anyway... that's that for this week. Later.
Sunday, 1/20/2002 @ 1:02 PM PST by: Mr.E
Catching Up...
Yes, I know...we've been updating very little recently. There's just been nothing to report. Anyhow, two more VA transcripts are available at the official site, in case you want to read them (Guess which webmaster who goes by a single letter doesn't! I bet you can't!).

That's all guys. I wish I could pull news out of my ass, but quite frankly I don't think you want to fish through my waste to know what I ate last night. OK, that was a little disgusting. I'm not going into the reasons why I'm not doing content updates again. I'm a little tired of telling it, and I'm sure you guys are sick of hearing it.

Anyhow...we're supposed to see the first in-game screenshots of the GBA cartridge really soon via a few popular gaming magazines, so that should be interesting. Let's hope it's not as crappy as the rumors have suggested.
Thursday, 1/17/2002 @ 1:08 PM PST by: Mr.E
Notice...
Guys, please stop sending me news about Yu Yu Hakusho/Blue Gender. This is a Dragon Ball site, and I have absolutely no interest in those two series. Plus, I don't feel like giving FUNimation free advertising for something I could care less about.

Also, spare us the Bid For Power news as well. It's not Dragon Ball-related anymore, so I do not care when it's being released. That is all.
Monday, 1/14/2002 @ 9:51 PM PST by: Jon Allen
New Feature: Enter Bruce Faulconer
Here it is everyone, my belated Christmas present, first major internet thing of 2002, and my first interview since "Settin' It Strait With Sonny" back on Daizenshuu EX in November of 2000. I spent a lot of time on it, so I hope that you'll all savor it like the taste of filet mignon in your mouth. I wish I could come up with more to say to hype it up, but alas, that would take away valuable reading time, and believe me, you're gonna need it for this.

So sit back and enjoy as we Enter bruce Faulconer.

Wait a minute...that didn't sound right, and it sucked. Let me try again.

So sit back and enjoy the literary achievment that is, was, and forever shall be, Enter Bruce Faulconer, the composer for Funimation's dub of Dragonball Z's current soundtrack.

There. Much better. Also, no I will not be translating that flash movie I linked you to. Words would ruin it's beauty. I appologize for Tripod consistently crashing, by the way. My bad.
Monday, 1/14/2002 @ 11:02 AM PST by: Jon Allen
More News On French DVDs
Here's part of an email I received from Sangofe.
Hi,I emailed Manga-Distribution about the DBZ dvds...First off, they are releasing the "light dbz version" only to get money so that they don't go on the negative side releasing the subbed dvds, apparently, they had to pay a lot of money for the rights..
He goes on to link me to a site that reviews French DVDs. After some work with a translator, I came up with the following.

  • There are no English subtitles, or plans to include them.

  • Incorrect openings and closing are used for the episodes.

  • the only chapter select is that you can either watch the whole episode, or skip to halfway through.

  • All audio tracks are in mono.

  • Video quality isn't as good.


    • And to recap what Sangofe said in the email, the only discs they have released so are the "light editions," which do not have the Japanese audio track. The "collectors editions" which do contain the Japanese audio have yet to be released, and in fact have been delayed indefinitely. If the Japanese audio is included, don't expect it to be anything 5 star either as they had planned to simply use the tapes they dubbed with, which have no doubt degraded in the years since the French dub was completed. Basically, from an English speaking DBZ otaku standpoint, if you were to go to the trouble to import these DVDs, you'd be doing so solely for the cover art. Not exactly the silver lining that it was once perceived.
